summary: Jam‑heavy Warfield set with fiery Scarlet>Fire and strong early‑'80s flow
blurb: The show delivers a jam‑heavy first set highlighted by a scorching Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain, while the second set features a classic Playing In The Band sandwich with Terrapin Station and Althea. Keyboard work shines on Friend Of The Devil, and fans note a possible Foxy Lady tease during Space.
review: Feb 16‑1982 at the Warfield captured the Dead in a solid early‑'80s groove. The first set rolls out with a tight Shakedown Street into a standout Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain that many listeners call “fiery” and jam‑heavy, while Friend Of The Devil gets a sweet keyboard touch from Brent. The second half builds a classic Playing In The Band sandwich, delivering Terrapin Station’s soaring climax, a rolling Althea, and an energetic Space where some fans even hear a fleeting Foxy Lady tease. Though the available audience recordings are only fair in clarity—muffled bass and occasional drum bleed—the performance itself remains enjoyable and historically interesting for its blend of cowboy‑style tunes and expansive improvisation.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: The four recording analyses are mostly mixed in tone with only one clearly positive note; there is no consistent praise or criticism across recordings, so sentiment adjustment remains neutral.

best_recording.reason: Captures the standout Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain sequence despite fair audio, and is praised for its immersive audience feel.

key_highlights: Fiery Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain jam
Classic Playing In The Band sandwich with Terrapin Station and Althea
Possible Foxy Lady tease woven into the Space jam

band_performance.Jerry: No specific remarks, but overall playing is described as solid and jam‑heavy.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work noted as sweet on 'Friend Of The Devil'.
